project_specification.overview: A minimal command-line TODO list in Python. Tasks are persisted in a local tasks.json file. Five features cover full CRUD: scaffold, add, list, mark done, and delete. This spec is the reference smoke-test fixture for the claw-forge-plugin; it is intentionally tiny so the full pipeline can complete within the 5-minute success-metric window.
project_specification.target_audience: claw-forge-plugin developers running the manual smoke test. The generated app is a validation target, not a product.

backend.storage: tasks.json flat file in the project root

communication.api_style: CLI — subcommands: add, list, done, delete

feature.description: Project contains todo.py as the CLI entry point with an argparse parser that registers add/list/done/delete subcommands; tasks.json is initialised to [] on first write (not committed); README.md documents each subcommand with a one-line usage example; running `python todo.py --help` exits 0 and lists all four subcommands

User can run `python todo.py add "buy milk"` and the task is appended to tasks.json with an auto-incremented integer id, the provided title, and done=false; the command prints "Added task #N: <title>" to stdout; running add when tasks.json is absent creates the file automatically

User can run `python todo.py list` and sees a formatted table where each row shows the task id, a status indicator ([ ] for pending, [x] for done), and the title; when tasks.json is empty or absent the command prints "No tasks yet." and exits 0

User can run `python todo.py done <id>` to set done=true on the task with the given integer id and persist the change to tasks.json; the command prints "Marked #N as done"; if the id does not exist the command prints "Task #N not found" to stderr and exits 1

User can run `python todo.py delete <id>` to remove the task with the given integer id from tasks.json; the command prints "Deleted task #N"; if the id does not exist the command prints "Task #N not found" to stderr and exits 1

success_criteria.functionality: All five features pass their described behaviour checks. The acceptance sequence below produces the expected output: python todo.py add "task one" # → Added task #1: task one python todo.py add "task two" # → Added task #2: task two python todo.py add "task three" # → Added task #3: task three python todo.py list # → 3 rows, all [ ] python todo.py done 2 # → Marked #2 as done python todo.py delete 1 # → Deleted task #1 python todo.py list # → 2 rows: #2 [x], #3 [ ] Error paths: `done 99` and `delete 99` print "Task #99 not found" and exit 1.
